definition: Helping people earn enough money for basic needs.
example: Like making sure everyone gets lunch.
Like growing a small shop into a big store.

definition: Roads, bridges, and buildings needed for society.
example: Like hallways and classrooms in school.

definition: Money or supplies given to help another country.
example: Like donating books to another class.
Like lending a friend money to start selling lemonade.

definition: Growing the economy while protecting nature.
example: Like using resources wisely.
Like building new playground equipment.
Like improving farms.

definition: Chances for people to earn money.
example: Like opportunities to work after school.
Like making sure every student has books.
Like upgrading the school nurse office.
Like having enough snacks for everyone.
Like starting new projects for students.

definition: Organized efforts to improve living conditions.
example: Like school improvement projects.

definition: Making sure everyone can participate in the economy.
example: Like letting every student join clubs.

definition: Spending money on roads or bridges.
example: Like fixing broken playground equipment.
Like planning how to grow a garden.
Like minimum points needed to pass a test.
Like class goals for better grades.
Like helping students grow smarter and healthier.

definition: Giving people power to improve their economy.
example: Like teaching students how to earn money.

definition: Improving buying and selling between countries.
example: Like students trading toys fairly.

definition: Sharing technology to help poorer countries.
example: Like sharing computer access with others.
Like learning how to use new tools.
Like improving your classroom together.
